<!-- Generated by StrongAIAutoDoc 20260524 -->

GuardDog is a Node.js/TypeScript command-line toolchain used by developers and CI operators to run repository-focused architecture, security, and quality review workflows. It scans a local Git repository, selects representative context (including C4 documentation), and uses an external prompt runtime plus an LLM provider to generate schema-constrained, structured findings and a Markdown/JSON report. Results are validated, filtered by thresholds and token budgets, and emitted with deterministic exit codes for automation. When configured, GuardDog can publish findings as GitHub issues in a target repository. The package also includes utilities for repository scanning, safe context loading, and token estimation to support consistent, repeatable reviews.

```mermaid
C4Context
title GuardDog (Package Root) - C4 Context

Person(dev, "Developer / Reviewer", "Runs GuardDog locally to initialize configuration and generate review outputs.")
Person(ci, "CI Operator / Pipeline", "Runs GuardDog non-interactively, evaluates exit codes, and collects artifacts.")

System_Boundary(sb, "GuardDog") {
  System(guarddog, "GuardDog CLI + Review Engine", "CLI workflows (init/review) that scan repos, select context, call an LLM for schema-constrained findings, validate/filter results, render reports, and optionally publish GitHub issues.")
}

System_Ext(repoFs, "Local Git Repository (Filesystem)", "Source code, configs, .guarddog/ artifacts, C4 docs, and outputs read/written during init and review.")
System_Ext(node, "Node.js Runtime", "Executes the CLI, provides process state, env vars, and console I/O.")
System_Ext(promptRuntime, "Prompt Repository Runtime (@jonverrier/prompt-repository)", "Loads bundled prompts (via stable prompt IDs) and provides chat driver/model abstraction.")
System_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider API", "Generates context ranking and architecture review results under JSON schema constraints.")
System_Ext(githubApi, "GitHub REST API", "Creates issues (and applies labels) based on findings when enabled.")
System_Ext(env, "Execution Environment", "Provides configuration such as GITHUB_TOKEN, OPENAI_MODEL, and GUARDDOG_DEBUG.")
System_Ext(tiktoken, "tiktoken Library", "Token encodings used to estimate prompt sizes and enforce token budgets.")

Rel(dev, guarddog, "Invokes init/review; reviews reports and logs", "Terminal / CLI args")
Rel(ci, guarddog, "Invokes review; checks exit codes; persists artifacts", "CI job execution")
Rel(guarddog, repoFs, "Scans repo; reads/writes .guarddog config and outputs", "Filesystem I/O")
Rel(guarddog, node, "Runs within; prints to stdout/stderr; exits with codes", "Node process")
Rel(guarddog, env, "Reads runtime configuration and secrets", "process.env")
Rel(guarddog, tiktoken, "Counts tokens to pack context within budgets", "Library API")
Rel(guarddog, promptRuntime, "Loads prompt templates by stable IDs", "Library API")
Rel(guarddog, llm, "Submits prompts; receives schema-constrained JSON results", "HTTPS")
Rel(guarddog, githubApi, "Optionally creates issues from findings", "HTTPS")
```

GuardDog is invoked by a developer or CI pipeline via the CLI, which parses arguments, dispatches `init` and `review` workflows, and communicates outcomes through stdout/stderr plus explicit process exit codes for automation. It reads and writes to the local repository filesystem: `init` scaffolds `.guarddog/` configuration and guidance files, while `review` scans the repository (respecting ignore rules), selects and safely loads context, and produces Markdown/JSON outputs. For token budgeting, GuardDog uses the external tiktoken library and model selection from environment configuration (e.g., OPENAI_MODEL). Review execution depends on a prompt runtime (@jonverrier/prompt-repository) that loads prompt templates referenced by stable IDs (as centralized in PromptIds) and abstracts chat driver/model selection. GuardDog then calls an external LLM provider over HTTPS to obtain schema-constrained JSON findings, validates and filters them against thresholds, and optionally publishes results as GitHub issues using the GitHub REST API with credentials such as GITHUB_TOKEN from the execution environment.
